name: Blend
what_it_does: Blend provides a digital lending platform API for mortgage and consumer loan origination, enabling lenders to embed point-of-sale loan applications, process applications, and manage the lending workflow programmatically.
use_cases: Embedding mortgage or consumer loan application flows into bank or fintech products
Automating loan application status tracking and decisioning workflow management
Integrating Blend's pre-fill and income verification into loan origination systems
Building co-branded digital lending experiences for lender customers
Automating document collection, verification, and underwriting workflow triggers
not_for: Non-bank lending (Blend requires licensed lender relationships)
Small business or commercial lending — Blend focuses on consumer and mortgage
Real-time consumer credit APIs without the lending origination context
Marketplaces that aggregate multiple lenders (use LendingTree or Lendio for that)
best_when: A bank, credit union, or licensed lender needs to digitize mortgage or consumer loan origination with a white-label application experience and workflow automation for underwriters.
avoid_when: You need a lender marketplace, small business loans, or don't have a licensed lender relationship — Blend requires institutional banking partners to access.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 for partner integrations. API keys available for simpler server-to-server use cases. Institutional lender credentials required — individual developers cannot access Blend APIs without a lender partner account.

pricing.notes: Enterprise licensing model. Blend charges lenders a platform fee (per-loan or annual license). API access is included in lender platform subscription — not sold separately to developers.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses are structured JSON but messages are often generic (e.g., 'validation error') without field-level detail. Loan status errors and decisioning rejections require Blend support to interpret in edge cases.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No documented idempotency key support. Loan application creation should be deduplicated at the application layer using applicant identifiers.
known_agent_gotchas: No self-service developer access — requires institutional lender partnership before API keys are issued
Loan application state machines are complex: draft, submitted, processing, conditionally approved, approved, denied, withdrawn — handle all states
FCRA and GLBA compliance obligations flow to the API consumer — agents must not store or misuse applicant data
Blend's API is tightly coupled to their UX platform; some workflows assume a human applicant in the loop
Webhook payloads for loan status changes can be large and nested — validate schema before processing
Income verification and pre-fill features depend on third-party integrations (Plaid, Finicity) with their own failure modes
Sandbox environment access is not publicly available — must request through Blend sales process

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC 2 Type II certified. PCI-DSS compliant. TLS enforced. GLBA-compliant data handling. OAuth 2.0 with partner-level scoping. Access restricted to credentialed institutional lenders — strong access control by design.
